title: Pass 5.C.5: ferrotorch-core cross() ignores dim parameter; full_meta ignores _value
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-core audit, STUB bucket (2 findings).

- cross() accepts a `dim` parameter and silently ignores it — always operates on the canonical axis. PyTorch parity broken: torch.cross(a, b, dim=-1) and dim=0 must produce different outputs on non-trivial shapes.
- full_meta accepts a _value parameter and silently ignores it — uses default fill instead.

Both are reachable from public API. (File:line cites in the core audit subagent report — see #982 rollup.)

Acceptance:
- cross(): respect the dim arg; add discriminating fixture covering at least dim=0 and dim=-1 on a non-square tensor
- full_meta: respect the _value arg; remove the underscore prefix indicating intentional-discard.
